Questions: In the study of the Nasoni Access-H2O smart faucet for individuals with spinal cord injury, how many subjects will be recruited?
reference_answer: 20 subjects will be recruited, specifically individuals living with spinal cord injury above C7.
reference_notes: Verified from the study overview/sample description.

Questions: For the study on Virtual Reality-based upper limb rehabilitation for tetraplegia, what is the primary objective?
reference_answer: The primary objective is to evaluate the feasibility of a co-designed, bespoke, high-immersion VR intervention for upper limb exercise during acute upper limb rehabilitation after spinal cord injury.
reference_notes: Verified from the Study aims section, Primary Objective: Feasibility.

Questions: According to the protocol for treating oral chronic GVHD with mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) dressing, how often is the dressing applied?
reference_answer: The MSC dressing is applied 4 times per day for 2 weeks.
reference_notes: Verified from Treatment Protocol, Methods of stem cell administration.

Questions: In the study comparing low-dose Emicizumab vs. low-dose Factor VIII for hemophilia A, what is the route of administration for Emicizumab?
reference_notes: Verified from the study procedure dosing description for low-dose emicizumab.

Questions: In the Access-H2O smart faucet feasibility study, name the three specific control methods being tested and list the two Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) participants will perform
reference_answer: The three control methods are voice, eye gaze, and machine vision. The two ADLs are drinking water and washing the face.
reference_notes: Verified from Study Procedures describing Access-H2O control methods and ADL trials.

Questions: In the isokinetic training study for stroke (NCT06153992), what angular velocities (in degrees/second) are used for training, and what is the "golden indicator" measured?
reference_answer: The angular velocities are 60 degrees/second, 90 degrees/second, and 120 degrees/second. The golden indicator is peak torque, measured at 60 degrees/second.
reference_notes: Verified from Materials and Methods and outcome assessment text.

Questions: In the Music Therapy for cognition study (NCT06156735), how will subjects' confidentiality be guarded, and how long will documents be kept before shredding?
reference_answer: Confidentiality is guarded by assigning subject IDs linked to names in a separate password-protected document accessible through a password-protected user account; physical documents are locked in a file cabinet in a locked office accessible only to the investigator. Documents are kept for two years before shredding.

Questions: According to the Hepatitis C screening protocol (NCT06155006), what is the minimum sample size required per institution with a 95% confidence interval, and what is the assumed hypothetical frequency of the outcome factor in the population?
reference_answer: The minimum sample size required per institution at a 95% confidence interval is 1,151. The assumed hypothetical frequency of the outcome factor is 50%.
reference_notes: Verified from Sample size section and Table 1.

Questions: What is the total sample size targeted for the postprandial ambulation study in gestational diabetes (NCT06157684), and why was an extra ~10% added?
reference_answer: The target sample size is 90 participants. The study originally required 41 participants per group, 82 total, and increased recruitment to 90 to account for an approximately 10% dropout rate.
